### Sheet: SEM
|   Unnamed: 0 | Back to READ ME                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |   Unnamed: 2 |   Unnamed: 3 | 'EEM 1' tab   |   Unnamed: 5 | Unnamed: 6                                                        |
|-------------:|:---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------:|-------------:|:--------------|-------------:|:------------------------------------------------------------------|
|          nan | This worksheet is not applicable to your project.  Please continue to the next tab (EEM1).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | California SEM Measure and Verification (M&V) Guide, Version 3.02 |
|          nan | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | Initial Project Submittal, Selection, and Disposition:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | • SEM projects seeking SEM-program based incentives should be included in the bi-monthly submission list at the end of the first reporting period.                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | • Projects not selected for CPR at time of initial listing do not need to submit a PFS, but will need to be listed again after the first reporting period of each new two-year cycle.                                                                                                                                                                                |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | • Projects selected for CPR:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | o Dispositions for these projects are advisory-only.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | o These projects need to submit a completed PFS within two weeks of CPR project selection.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | o These projects do not need to be relisted on the bi-monthly submission list in any subsequent two-year cycle.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | o For projects that do not have viable models or calculations indicating savings within the first year, CPUC may request additional documentation beyond the first year.                                                                                                                                                                                             |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | The project documentation package (submitted along with the PFS) must include:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | Documentation type                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |          nan |          nan | File name(s)  |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | Year 1 Completion Report (Reporting Performance Period Report - RPPR):                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | Documentation that meets the requirements of Section 14.1.2 through Section 14.1.7 of the M&V Guide shall be provided.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|          nan | Opportunity Register:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | Includes energy performance and Energy Management System (EnMS) improvement opportunities identified and completed through SEM engagement.                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|          nan | ((If a bottom-up approach is being sought))                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | Notification of Bottom-up Method of Determining Energy Savings (NOBU):                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|              | The NOBU should contain:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|          nan | • Statement describing efforts taken to-date to create energy consumption adjustment models.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | • Justification for not further pursuing energy consumption adjustment models and switching to the bottom-up approach.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | • Discussion of what efforts can and will be taken to enable the development of energy consumption adjustment models in subsequent Reporting Periods.                                                                                                                                                                                                                |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | Model or Savings Calculation:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | If claiming savings for either top-down or bottom-up savings calculations. File should include granular production and meter data (not just cumulative data).                                                                                                                                                                                                        |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|          nan | Scoping Summary:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | This document describes the conditions that existed at each site prior to SEM Engagement, focused primarily on energy efficiency history and plans.                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|          nan | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | As available, additional documentation may include:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | Documentation type                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |          nan |          nan | File name(s)  |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | Treasure Hunt Summary:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | Provides details on the outcomes of the treasure hunt.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|          nan | Energy Data Collection Plan:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | Provides information describing when and how data should be collected from identified data sources. It will address the collection of energy consumption and relevant variable data.                                                                                                                                                                                 |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|          nan | Energy Savings Report:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | If applicable and separate from RPPR. Includes a more detailed description of the statistical validity of the model.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|          nan | Technical Review:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | If applicable. Sometimes the claimed savings overrides the savings from the model or bottom-up calculations. This document provides support for the changes to modeled or calculated savings.                                                                                                                                                                        |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|          nan | Greenhouse Gas and IDSM Summary:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | If available, from workshop summaries. Documentation indicating that the customer has conveyed their interest, or not, regarding the reduction of greenhouse gases and pursuing integrated demand-side management (IDSM) measures and is informed how the SEM M&V process can help their reporting needs if they choose to pursue either of these kinds of measures. |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|          nan | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |

| nan                                                                                                                              | Standard Practice                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | nan                                                                   | nan          | nan          | nan                   | nan                                                                       | nan                        | nan                                                                | nan                | Assessment of standard practice may be required for measure eligibility, for example to demonstrate compliance with Assembly Bill 802’s requirement that the measure will meet or exceed code. | nan                                                                                                                              | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | nan                                                                   | nan          | nan          | nan                   | nan                                                                       | nan                        | nan                                                                | nan                | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | ISP Guidance Document                                              | nan                                                                 |
| nan                                                                                                                              | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | nan                                                                   | nan          | nan          | nan                   | nan                                                                       | nan                        | nan                                                                | nan                | Describe any applicable industry standard practice study or previously approved standard practice baseline associated with this baseline/ measure. If a code is the baseline, make a direct reference to the building energy efficiency standards or other energy-related code by section and table number.                                                                                             | nan                                                                | nan                                                                 |

| nan                                                                                                                              | Is this a fuel substitution measure?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | nan                                                                   | nan          | nan          | nan                   | nan                                                                       | nan                        | nan                                                                | nan                | Use the RACC-FSC_v3.0 to calculate source energy savings and CO2 offsets for this measure.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | RACC-FSC_v3.0 / Technical Guidance Document(s)                     | nan                                                                 |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  | Per the RACC-FSC Technical Guidance Document:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    | For custom (i.e., CIAC) measures, please see:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  | "Fuel substitution measures, in the context of energy efficiency programs, involve energy efficiency projects where all or a portion of the existing energy use is converted from one fuel to another (i.e., natural gas to electricity or vice versa). Only equipment powered by electricity and/or natural gas fuels and provided by a CPUC-regulated investor-owned utility or a municipal utility are eligible to participate under fuel substitution measures. Measures involving non-utility (unregulated) fuels, such as propane or fuel oil, are termed as fuel switching measures. " |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |  • columns DO:DT of the '2 RACC' worksheet for avoided refirergant costs/benefits                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |  • columns BT:CT of the '3 FSC' worksheet for the energy savings and avoided emissions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                    |                                                                     |
| nan                                                                                                                              | Does this measure involve adding or replacing equipment that uses refrigerant and involve a change to the refrigerant type / charge?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | nan                                                                   | nan          | nan          | nan                   | nan                                                                       | nan                        | nan                                                                | nan                | Use the RACC-FSC_v3.0 to calculate refrigerant leakage emissions and avoided costs of net refrigerant leakage emissions for this measure.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | nan                                                                | nan                                                                 |
| nan                                                                                                                              | If applicable, what type of refrigerant does the existing equipment use?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | nan                                                                   | nan          | nan          | nan                   | nan                                                                       | nan                        | nan                                                                | nan                | Select the type(s) of refrigerant(s) from the drop town menu for both the existing and proposed equipment.  Select 'Not applicable' for the existing refrigerant type if this measure only involves adding equipment that uses refrigerant.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | nan                                                                | nan                                                                 |
| nan                                                                                                                              | What type of refrigerant will the proposed equipment use?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | nan                                                                   | nan          | nan          | nan                   | nan                                                                       | nan                        | nan                                                                | nan                | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | nan                                                                | nan                                                                 |

| nan                                                                                                                              | Energy Usage                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | 1st Period Baseline Usage                                             | kWh          | nan          | nan                   | NA - please include supporting file for calculation methodology in Row 43 | nan                        | NA - include supporting file for calculation methodology in Row 38 | nan                | Please provide the following details and a description of calculation methodology in the supporting file referenced in cell(s) E43:G43:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | nan                                                                | nan                                                                 |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |   • Supporting file number(s) for calculation methodology in Row 43                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |   • Comprehensive details on inputs (i.e., assumptions, equations, metered data, spec sheet, energy saving principles)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |   • References to any study and data source and notes on all assumptions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |   • Complete calibration of energy simulation models (if applicable)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    | For custom spreadsheet analyses, the calculation methodology must be clearly presented and summarized in a single place (e.g., dedicated tab in a workbook / spreadsheet).  It is not realistic to expect that a technical reviewer will search cell by cell, tab by tab through a spreadsheet to ascertain how savings impacts are calculated.                                                                                                                                      |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    | Initial estimates of a custom measure’s savings impacts are often performed using “placeholder” calculations which are estimates based on accepted engineering principals and assumptions, the basis of which must be clearly documented and reasonable. For many custom projects, the final savings claims estimates are based on pre-installation and post-installation measurements with a savings calculation methodology that is different than the “placeholder” calculations. |                                                                    |                                                                     |

| nan                                                                                                                              | Is this a fuel substitution measure?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | nan                                                                   | nan          | nan          | nan                   | nan                                                                       | nan                        | nan                                                                | nan                | Use the RACC-FSC_v3.0 to calculate source energy savings and CO2 offsets for this measure.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | RACC-FSC_v3.0 / Technical Guidance Document(s)                     | nan                                                                 |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  | Per the RACC-FSC Technical Guidance Document:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    | For custom (i.e., CIAC) measures, please see:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  | "Fuel substitution measures, in the context of energy efficiency programs, involve energy efficiency projects where all or a portion of the existing energy use is converted from one fuel to another (i.e., natural gas to electricity or vice versa). Only equipment powered by electricity and/or natural gas fuels and provided by a CPUC-regulated investor-owned utility or a municipal utility are eligible to participate under fuel substitution measures. Measures involving non-utility (unregulated) fuels, such as propane or fuel oil, are termed as fuel switching measures. " |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |  • columns DO:DT of the '2 RACC' worksheet for avoided refirergant costs/benefits                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |  • columns BT:CT of the '3 FSC' worksheet for the energy savings and avoided emissions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                    |                                                                     |
| nan                                                                                                                              | Does this measure involve adding or replacing equipment that uses refrigerant and involve a change to the refrigerant type / charge?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | nan                                                                   | nan          | nan          | nan                   | nan                                                                       | nan                        | nan                                                                | nan                | Use the RACC-FSC_v3.0 to calculate refrigerant leakage emissions and avoided costs of net refrigerant leakage emissions for this measure.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | nan                                                                | nan                                                                 |
| nan                                                                                                                              | If applicable, what type of refrigerant does the existing equipment use?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | nan                                                                   | nan          | nan          | nan                   | nan                                                                       | nan                        | nan                                                                | nan                | Select the type(s) of refrigerant(s) from the drop town menu for both the existing and proposed equipment.  Select 'Not applicable' for the existing refrigerant type if this measure only involves adding equipment that uses refrigerant.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | nan                                                                | nan                                                                 |
| nan                                                                                                                              | What type of refrigerant will the proposed equipment use?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | nan                                                                   | nan          | nan          | nan                   | nan                                                                       | nan                        | nan                                                                | nan                | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | nan                                                                | nan                                                                 |
